title: Demo Agency Adjudication Manual — § 4.21 Secondary Causation Theories
passage: Adjudicators must develop and address each theory of secondary causation raised by the record, including theories raised by the claimant's submissions or apparent on the face of the medical evidence. Failure to address a credibly raised theory is a procedural error.

title: Demo Agency Adjudication Manual — § 6.05 Use of Lay Evidence
passage: Lay statements describing observable symptoms must be considered and may be sufficient, alone, to establish continuity of symptomatology when corroborated by the broader record.
